vector-metric.label: Vector & metric distances
vector-metric.blurb: Classical distances between points in R^n: Euclidean, Manhattan, Minkowski, Chebyshev, Mahalanobis and friends. Most are true metrics.

information-geometry.label: Riemannian & information geometry
information-geometry.blurb: Distance as the length of the shortest path (geodesic) on a curved space; for statistical models the curvature comes from the Fisher information.

f-divergence.label: f-divergences (Csiszar / Ali-Silvey)
f-divergence.blurb: A master family generated by a convex function f with f(1)=0. Contains KL, chi-square, Hellinger and total variation. Only TV is a metric.

bregman.label: Bregman divergences
bregman.blurb: Generated by a strictly convex potential F: the gap between F and its tangent plane. Generally asymmetric; arithmetic mean is the Bregman centroid.

matrix-bregman.label: Matrix Bregman & matrix distances
matrix-bregman.blurb: Bregman divergences and norms lifted from vectors to matrices via a spectral seed and the trace inner product (Frobenius, von Neumann, Log-Det, spectral).

overlap-affinity.label: Overlap / alpha-power family
overlap-affinity.blurb: All read off the affinity integral of p^alpha q^(1-alpha): Bhattacharyya, Chernoff information and Renyi divergence.

symmetrized.label: Symmetrized & Jensen-type
symmetrized.blurb: Symmetric divergences built by averaging or symmetrizing: Jeffreys (symmetric KL), Jensen-Shannon, and Burbea-Rao.

entropy.label: Entropies
entropy.blurb: Functionals of a single distribution that quantify uncertainty; the building blocks behind many divergences.

set-metric-space.label: Set & metric-space distances
set-metric-space.blurb: Distances between sets or whole metric spaces: Hausdorff (sets) and Gromov-Hausdorff (spaces up to isometry).

optimal-transport.label: Optimal transport
optimal-transport.blurb: Distances defined by the minimum cost of moving mass under a ground geometry: Wasserstein / Earth Mover's Distance and the Bures-Wasserstein metric.

ipm.label: Integral probability metrics (IPMs)
ipm.blurb: Distance as the largest gap a test function from a class can produce: Wasserstein-1, MMD, Kolmogorov-Smirnov, Levy-Prokhorov, Stein discrepancy.

spd-matrix.label: SPD / covariance-matrix geometry
spd-matrix.blurb: Distances tailored to symmetric positive-definite (covariance/kernel) matrices: affine-invariant Riemannian, Bures-Wasserstein, Log-Det, Frobenius.

quantum.label: Quantum geometry
quantum.blurb: Density-matrix analogues where integrals become traces: von Neumann entropy and the von Neumann (quantum relative) divergence.

kernel-representation.label: Kernel & representation similarity
kernel-representation.blurb: Similarities between kernel/Gram matrices or learned representations, e.g. Centered Kernel Alignment (CKA).

graph-distance.label: Graph distances
graph-distance.blurb: Distances between graphs: structural (graph edit distance) or spectral (comparing Laplacian eigenvalues).
